summary: The parotid duct (Stensen's duct) opens opposite the upper second molar tooth into the vestibule of the mouth.
The parotid duct opens opposite the lower first molar tooth.
The parotid duct opens on the sublingual papilla beside the frenulum.
General sensation to the anterior two-thirds of the tongue is supplied by the lingual nerve (CN V3), while taste is carried by the chorda tympani (CN VII).
Both sensation and taste to the anterior two-thirds are supplied by the glossopharyngeal nerve.
The hypoglossal nerve provides both sensation and taste to the entire tongue.
The glossopharyngeal nerve (CN IX) supplies both general sensation and taste to the posterior one-third of the tongue.
The vagus nerve supplies sensation and taste to the posterior one-third of the tongue.
The lingual nerve supplies the posterior one-third of the tongue.
All muscles of the tongue are supplied by the hypoglossal nerve (CN XII) except palatoglossus, which is supplied by the vagus nerve (CN X).
All tongue muscles without exception are supplied by the hypoglossal nerve.
Palatoglossus is supplied by the glossopharyngeal nerve (CN IX).
Genioglossus is the primary protrusor of the tongue; unilateral paralysis causes the tongue to deviate toward the affected side.
Hyoglossus is the primary protrusor of the tongue.
Unilateral hypoglossal nerve palsy causes the tongue to deviate to the opposite side.
Waldeyer's lymphatic ring consists of the pharyngeal, tubal, palatine, and lingual tonsils arranged as a ring of lymphoid tissue at the pharyngeal entrance.
Waldeyer's ring consists only of the palatine tonsils.
Waldeyer's ring is located in the larynx, not the pharynx.
Killian's dehiscence is the weak area between the thyropharyngeus and cricopharyngeus parts of the inferior constrictor, where Zenker's diverticulum herniates.
Killian's dehiscence is located in the superior constrictor muscle.
Zenker's diverticulum herniates through a defect in the middle constrictor.
The soft palate contains five muscles: tensor veli palatini, levator veli palatini, palatoglossus, palatopharyngeus, and musculus uvulae.
The soft palate contains only three muscles.
All muscles of the soft palate are supplied by the mandibular nerve (CN V3).
